summary: Gruber discusses the process of learning and using regular expressions, prompted by Dr. Drang's post about Jason Snell's BBEdit workflow. He explains why interactive text editing in BBEdit is often preferable to writing scripts for one-off tasks, emphasizing the value of instant visual feedback. He also reveals his role in writing the PCRE-related portions of BBEdit's Grep chapter in the user manual, which both he and Drang learned regex from. The post advocates for BBEdit's manual as the best starting point for regex beginners, noting that a 500-page book like Friedl's can be intimidating and unnecessary for getting started.
key_points: For one-off text transformations, working interactively in BBEdit with regex is faster and more practical than writing a script
Even when writing automation scripts, Gruber starts by working out regex transformations step-by-step in BBEdit for instant visual feedback
BBEdit's Grep chapter in its user manual is recommended as the best regex tutorial for beginners, superior to diving into Friedl's comprehensive 500-page book
Gruber pushed for BBEdit to switch from a basic regex engine to Philip Hazel's PCRE library, and wrote the manual sections covering PCRE's advanced features
The regex syntax described in BBEdit's manual is portable and works in virtually every modern programming language or text editor
money_quotes: If the task at hand is something I only need to do once or twice, right now, it's simply easier to just do it in BBEdit.
Instant visual feedback with undo support — I've worked with text this way since 1992.
A 500+ page book to learn how to search for text? No thanks.
I went from 'this stuff looks like gibberish' to 'Oh, I get it, I see how this could be super useful' just by reading that chapter.
The regex syntax it describes will work in just about every current programming language or text editor.
I'm only going to make a proper script if it's something I know or suspect I'll reuse.
key_insight: The best way to learn regular expressions is not through comprehensive reference books but through practical, interactive experimentation with immediate visual feedback in a good text editor.
